title: My Anxiety First Aid Card
description: Create your personal anxiety first aid card — a quick-reference tool with your go-to coping strategies. Fill it in now, then download it to carry with you.
instructions: Think back through what you've learned in this course. Write down the specific techniques and resources that work best for you. This becomes your personal 'emergency kit' for anxious moments.

items.label: When I feel anxious, my breathing technique is:
items.placeholder: e.g., Box breathing (4-4-4-4), diaphragmatic breathing, 4-7-8 breathing

items.label: My grounding technique is:
items.placeholder: e.g., 5-4-3-2-1 senses exercise, cold water on wrists, feet on the ground

items.label: My coping thought is:
items.placeholder: e.g., 'This feeling is temporary and I have handled it before', 'Anxiety is uncomfortable but not dangerous'

items.label: My support person I can call or text:
items.placeholder: e.g., My partner Sarah (555-0123), My friend James, My therapist Dr. Lee

items.label: What has helped me before:
items.placeholder: e.g., Going for a walk, talking to someone, journaling, playing with my dog

disclaimer: This card is a personal coping tool. If you are in crisis, contact the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline by calling or texting 988, or text HOME to 741741 for the Crisis Text Line.
